item.title: Torch-Cutting Headlight Clearance Holes
item.description: Precision torch work is used to cut clearance holes for headlights during a fabrication project. How do fabricators keep cuts accurate on heavy steel?

item.title: Fixing a 1970s Yard Crane
item.description: A vintage yard crane from the 1970s undergoes repairs and restoration work to get it functioning again. What challenges come with reviving old equipment?

item.title: Fitting a new sleeve in a D10 track adjuster
item.description: A new sleeve is carefully fitted into a D10 track adjuster to restore proper function and durability. Why is precision so important in heavy equipment repairs?

item.title: What Is Air Arc Gouging?
item.description: A look at the air arc gouging process and how it’s used to remove metal in heavy fabrication and repair work. What makes this technique useful in workshops?
